_doc: Directive-count ratchet baseline (backlog #69 / Strand D, AC-11, docs/specs/directive-enforcement-audit.md). Per-file hard-directive keyword-hit counts for the four phase-entry surfaces, produced by counting `pattern` (tests/ci/test_directive_count_ratchet.py::count_directives) against each file with CRLF/CR normalized to LF first. GROWTH beyond a file's committed count is drift: test_real_surfaces_within_baseline FAILs. Equal or lower always passes — lowering the count is advisory (baseline could be ratcheted down) and requires a deliberate re-run of the counting function to update this file, mirroring the repo's lifecycle-baseline.json / update_lifecycle_baseline.py cap-at-today pattern.
note: cap-at-today ratchet; lower-only; not a target; keyword rewording can evade - targets drift, not adversaries
